ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'taco' class (FOODON_00002473); no lamb-specific taco subclass exists. Closest parent match used as lamb tacos is a specific variant of the taco food class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW covers taco as a composite dish; no lamb-specific variant code exists. Closest parent match used as lamb tacos is a specific preparation of the taco category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for lamb tacos or taco as a food item in the clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of lamb tacos is 2 tacos (approximately 200g), each consisting of a corn tortilla shell filled with seasoned ground lamb, shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, onions, and sour cream.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb Tacos — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database (F05020060)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database record F05020060 for Lamb Tacos. All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken as-is. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for ground lamb (cooked) and corn tortilla composite, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this record. Remaining micronutrients set to -1 where not reported by HPB or USDA for this composite dish.

gi_source.title: Corn tortilla GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for lamb tacos as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: corn tortilla (GI ~52, per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables). The protein (lamb, ~11g/100g) and fat (~11.8g/100g) components attenuate the glycaemic response, supporting a low-to-medium GI estimate. GL per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 x 25.84g) / 100 approximately 13.4 for a 200g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 274mg/100g — borderline range; typical 2-taco serving delivers approximately 548mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit lamb tacos to 1–2 tacos per meal, avoid additional high-sodium condiments such as salsa or cheese, and request reduced sour cream to keep total meal sodium below 600mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 12.9g/100g with moderate sugar 4.5g/100g; corn tortilla is a moderate-GI refined carbohydrate (GI approximately 52)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion to 1 taco, choose whole-grain corn tortillas where available, and increase non-starchy vegetable fillings to reduce glycaemic load and blunt postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 4.5g/100g from lamb and sour cream; trace trans fat approximately 0.1g/100g; sodium 274mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit lamb tacos to 1 taco per meal, substitute lamb with lean chicken or fish to reduce saturated fat, omit sour cream to lower saturated fat and sodium, and avoid adding high-sodium condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6); saturated fat approximately 4.5g/100g; trace trans fat approximately 0.1g/100g; nightshade ingredients (tomato, chilli powder)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit lamb tacos due to the pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid load from lamb, trace trans fat, and potential nightshade sensitivity from tomato; substituting lamb with grilled fish and omitting nightshade ingredients is strongly recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lamb tacos are a popular Mexican-inspired composite dish, typically featuring soft corn tortilla shells generously filled with seasoned ground lamb meat. The filling is complemented by fresh, crisp ingredients such as shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, and finely chopped onions, all topped with a dollop of creamy sour cream. The dish offers a savory and slightly tangy flavor profile, with the tender lamb contrasting with the fresh vegetables and soft tortilla. Nutritionally, lamb tacos provide 198 kcal per 100g, with a balanced macronutrient profile of 11.03g protein, 11.84g fat (including approximately 4.5g saturated fat and a trace 0.1g trans fat from ruminant lamb fat), and 12.92g carbohydrates, of which 4.54g are sugars and 2.06g are dietary fiber. The sodium content is 274mg per 100g. The corn tortilla contributes to a low-to-medium glycemic index (estimated at 52 based on University of Sydney GI Database data for corn tortilla). Clinically, the moderate sodium and saturated fat content necessitate portion control for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. For Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate glycemic index of the corn tortilla, combined with sugars, suggests a potential for glucose spikes, especially with typical serving sizes of 2 to 3 tacos. The lamb's arachidonic acid content and the presence of nightshade ingredients (tomato, chili powder) make this dish a high concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to potential inflammatory effects. The dish is naturally gluten-free when using corn tortillas but contains dairy from the sour cream, making it unsuitable for vegans or those with dairy allergies without modification. A standard serving of 2 tacos (approximately 200g) delivers approximately 396 kcal, 22g protein, 23.7g fat, and 25.8g carbohydrates.
common_user_queries: How many calories in lamb tacos?
Is lamb taco high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Foodo database record F05020060 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite data for ground lamb and corn tortilla. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (corn tortilla, Atkinson et al. 2008). All primary values are from approved sources.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB or USDA for this composite dish): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: HPB Foodo record F05020060 does not report these micronutrients for this composite dish, and USDA FoodData Central does not have an exact lamb taco composite entry with full micronutrient breakdown. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on USDA FoodData Central data for cooked ground lamb (trace trans fat from ruminant fat). saturated_fat_g set to 4.5 estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground lamb (cooked) and corn tortilla composite proportions. Occasion tag 'breakfast' from input cultural data is not supported for tacos and has been reclassified to lunch/dinner in meal_context. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' and food_subgroup 'Bread-based dishes' are preserved as the corn tortilla shell is the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Mexican dish; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese mandatory language rules apply. G6 multilingual compliance is satisfied as Mexican cuisine has no mandatory non-English language requirement under the Veritas multilingual matrix. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as the available codes represent the parent taco category rather than the specific lamb taco variant.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed from input JSON. Core macros, GI, health context, ontology, and aliases populated.
Iteration 2: Ontology match types were set to 'broader' which was not in the validator-allowed list. Attempted correction but validator still rejected.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent', which is explicitly listed in the validator-allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. Updated gap notes to reflect closest_parent terminology. All other sections preserved from iteration 2 as they passed all quality criteria.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
